display.purpose: Hero title on home page only. Tight tracking for confident presence.

headline.purpose: Page titles (Jobs, Settings). Bold but not heavy.

body.purpose: Descriptions, instructions, help text. Comfortable reading at 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Mono font for status badges, form labels, technical metadata. Uppercase with letter-spacing.

mono.purpose: Technical data in prose context — file names, model names, bitrates.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions. No bounce, no elastic.

motion.name: ease-out-quick
motion.purpose: Quick transitions for hover states (buttons, nav links).

motion.name: ease-out-standard
motion.purpose: Standard transitions for interactive feedback (upload zone, cards).

motion.name: ease-out-slow
motion.purpose: Slow transitions for progress indicators and state changes.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Accent-colored primary action button with mono label. Used for upload, play, download actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background-color: #6c63ff; color: #fff; padding: 10px 24px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; font-size: 0.85rem; font-weight: 600; font-family: 'SF Mono', 'Fira Code', 'Cascadia Code', monospace; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; cursor: pointer; transition: filter 0.2s ease, transform 0.1s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.15); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #6c63ff; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Subtle outlined button for less-emphasis actions like refresh or cancel.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05); color: #e2e8f0; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid #4a5568; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 0.8rem; font-weight: 600; font-family: 'SF Mono', 'Fira Code', 'Cascadia Code', monospace; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); } .ds-btn-secondary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: wait; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #6c63ff; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Destructive Button
components.description: Error-toned button for destructive actions like delete.
components.css: .ds-btn-destructive { background-color: rgba(252, 129, 129, 0.08); color: #fc8181; padding: 10px 20px; border: 1px solid rgba(252, 129, 129, 0.3); border-radius: 8px; font-size: 0.85rem; font-weight: 600; font-family: 'SF Mono', 'Fira Code', 'Cascadia Code', monospace; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-destructive:hover { background-color: rgba(252, 129, 129, 0.15); } .ds-btn-destructive:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #fc8181; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card Surface
components.description: Standard card container for stem players, job cards, and settings panels.
components.html:
Stem Name

Description text goes here.
components.css: .ds-card-surface { background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.03); border: 1px solid #4a5568; border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px; transition: border-color 0.2s ease; } .ds-card-surface:hover { border-color: rgba(108, 99, 255, 0.4); } .ds-card-title { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; color: #e2e8f0; margin: 0 0 8px; } .ds-card-text { font-size: 0.875rem; color: #a0aec0; margin: 0; }

components.name: Upload Zone
components.description: Drag-and-drop upload area with dashed border and icon. The signature component of Sound Splittr.
components.html:
Drop your song here

or click to browse
components.css: .ds-upload-zone { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 12px; padding: 48px 32px; border: 2px dashed #4a5568; border-radius: 12px; background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.03); transition: all 0.2s ease; cursor: pointer; } .ds-upload-zone:hover, .ds-upload-zone.dragging { border-color: #6c63ff; background-color: rgba(108, 99, 255, 0.08); } .ds-upload-zone.dragging .ds-upload-icon { transform: scale(1.1); } .ds-upload-icon { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 72px; height: 72px; border-radius: 50%; background-color: rgba(108, 99, 255, 0.1); color: #6c63ff; transition: transform 0.2s ease; } .ds-upload-icon svg { width: 32px; height: 32px; } .ds-upload-text { margin: 0; font-size: 1.1rem; font-weight: 600; color: #e2e8f0; } .ds-upload-sub { margin: 0; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #a0aec0; } .ds-upload-btn { display: inline-block; padding: 10px 24px; margin-top: 8px; font-size: 0.85rem; font-weight: 600; font-family: 'SF Mono', 'Fira Code', 'Cascadia Code', monospace; letter-spacing: 0.05em; color: #fff; background-color: #6c63ff; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: filter 0.2s ease, transform 0.1s ease; } .ds-upload-btn:hover { filter: brightness(1.15); } .ds-upload-btn:active { transform: scale(0.97); }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Text input for API URL, file paths, and settings values.
components.css: .ds-input { padding: 10px 12px; font-size: 0.9rem; color: #e2e8f0; background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3); border: 1px solid #4a5568; border-radius: 8px; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.2s ease; font-family: 'SF Mono', 'Fira Code', 'Cascadia Code', monospace; width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #718096; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #6c63ff; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #6c63ff; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Small rounded badge for job status indicators. Color-coded by state.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-block; padding: 2px 8px; font-size: 0.7rem; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; border-radius: 4px; color: #e2e8f0; } .ds-badge-queued { background-color: rgba(236, 201, 75, 0.15); } .ds-badge-processing { background-color: rgba(108, 99, 255, 0.15); } .ds-badge-completed { background-color: rgba(72, 187, 120, 0.15); } .ds-badge-failed { background-color: rgba(252, 129, 129, 0.15); }

components.name: Navigation Bar
components.description: Sticky top navigation with brand link and page nav links. Glass effect on the nav bar only.
components.html:
🎚️ Sound Splittr
HomeJobsSettings
components.css: .ds-nav { position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 100; padding: 0 24px; font-size: 0.9rem; background-color: rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.95); border-bottom: 1px solid #4a5568; backdrop-filter: blur(8px); } .ds-nav-inner { max-width: 1400px; margin: 0 auto; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; height: 56px; } .ds-nav-brand { font-size: 1.15rem; font-weight: 700; color: #6c63ff; text-decoration: none; transition: filter 0.2s ease; } .ds-nav-brand:hover { filter: brightness(1.2); } .ds-nav-links { display: flex; gap: 4px; } .ds-nav-link { padding: 8px 16px; color: #a0aec0; text-decoration: none; border-radius: 6px; transition: all 0.15s ease; } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #e2e8f0; background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05); } .ds-nav-link.active { color: #fff; background-color: rgba(108, 99, 255, 0.2); }

components.name: Progress Ring
components.description: Circular SVG progress indicator for processing jobs. Color adapts to state.
components.css: .ds-progress-ring { position: relative; width: 120px; height: 120px; } .ds-ring { width: 100%; height: 100%; transform: rotate(-90deg); } .ds-ring-bg { fill: none; stroke: #4a5568; stroke-width: 6; } .ds-ring-progress { fill: none; stroke: #6c63ff; stroke-width: 6; stroke-linecap: round; transition: stroke-dashoffset 0.4s ease; } .ds-ring-text { position: absolute; inset: 0; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 700; color: #6c63ff; transform: none; } .ds-progress-ring.complete .ds-ring-progress { stroke: #48bb78; } .ds-progress-ring.complete .ds-ring-text { color: #48bb78; } .ds-progress-ring.failed .ds-ring-progress { stroke: #fc8181; } .ds-progress-ring.failed .ds-ring-text { color: #fc8181; }

components.name: Range Slider
components.description: Custom range slider for volume and seek controls. Accent thumb on muted track.
components.css: .ds-range { flex: 1; height: 4px; -webkit-appearance: none; appearance: none; border-radius: 2px; background-color: #4a5568; outline: none; } .ds-range::-webkit-slider-thumb { -webkit-appearance: none; width: 14px; height: 14px; border-radius: 50%; background-color: #6c63ff; cursor: pointer; } .ds-range::-moz-range-thumb { width: 14px; height: 14px; border-radius: 50%; background-color: #6c63ff; border: none; cursor: pointer; } .ds-range::-webkit-slider-runnable-track { height: 4px; border-radius: 2px; background-color: #4a5568; }

narrative.overview:
Sound Splittr is a DJ's audio control room — the interface of a well-built piece of studio hardware. Dark by default because that's where musicians live, technical in its language, transparent about what it's doing. Every element should feel like a knob, a meter, or a fader on a professional mixing console: purposeful, tactile, honest.

The system rejects corporate SaaS aesthetics — no gradient blobs, no 'trust the process' copy, no stock-photo warmth. Instead it draws from the visual language of audio interfaces: Ableton's functional clarity, terminal tools' transparency, the satisfaction of seeing a waveform respond to your touch. There's warmth through the purple accent and subtle hover states, never through decorative fluff.
keyCharacteristics: Dark canvas, purple accent — the surface is deep slate, the accent fires sparingly on interactive elements
Mono for data, sans-serif for prose — monospace labels carry technical information; Inter carries the interface
Tonal layering over shadows — depth comes from subtle background shifts, not drop shadows
Inline settings, not hidden toggles — transparency is a principle, not a feature flag
Micro-interactions that respond — hover brightens, active scales down, transitions ease out

rules.name: The Accent Is Rare Rule
rules.body: The purple accent (#6c63ff) appears on no more than 10% of any given screen. Its scarcity makes it meaningful — when you see it, something is interactive or active.

rules.name: The No-Pure-White Rule
rules.body: Never use #fff for text or backgrounds. Tint everything toward the slate family. Pure white on a dark canvas is harsh and breaks the immersion of the control room.

rules.name: The Mono-For-Machine Rule
rules.body: Monospace font is reserved for data that comes from the system: file names, timestamps, model names, bitrates, GPU status. Human-written prose always uses Inter.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Never use box-shadow for elevation. This system is flat by default; shadows feel decorative on a dark canvas. Use tonal layering (background tints) for depth.

rules.name: The Dashed-Border Rule
rules.body: Dashed borders are reserved exclusively for action zones (upload areas). Solid borders are for containers. If you see a solid border on an upload zone or a dashed border on a card, you've broken the visual language.

dos: Do use the purple accent sparingly — on interactive elements and progress indicators only.
Do reserve monospace font for machine data: file names, timestamps, model names, bitrates, GPU status.
Do use tonal layering (background tints) for depth, not drop shadows.
Do keep borders at 1px for containers and 2px dashed for action zones.
Do use filter: brightness(1.15) for hover states on accent-colored elements.
Do use transform: scale(0.97) for active/press states.
Do use ease-out transitions (0.15s-0.4s range). No bounce, no elastic.
Do use semantic colors with background tints at 15% opacity for status indicators.
donts: Don't use box-shadow for elevation. This system is flat by default.
Don't use #fff for text or backgrounds. Always tint toward the slate family.
Don't use solid fills for semantic colors. Background tints at 15% opacity are the standard.
Don't use more than one saturated color on screen.
Don't put settings behind 'Advanced' toggles. Transparency is a principle.
Don't use gradient backgrounds on cards or buttons.
Don't use glassmorphism except on the navigation bar.
Don't use side-stripe borders on cards or list items (except job status indicators).
Don't use identical card grids. Vary padding, gap, and card sizes based on content density.
